summary: Tested new self-service profile update feature (POST /api/auth/update-profile) end-to-end. All 9 backend pytest cases and full UI flow passed. Regression checks for superadmin login, change-password, and 2FA setup all green.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: React console warning 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component' triggered by Login page (unrelated to this feature but should be fixed - likely a setState in render inside Login.jsx or AuthContext).
Suggestion: update-profile currently allows empty body (no-op returns current user). Consider returning 400 when neither name nor email provided, though this is a minor UX nit.
seed_data_creation: Created ephemeral TEST_ admin per test via POST /api/admins, deleted in teardown. No residual test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_update_profile.py. Note Pydantic EmailStr rejects .test/.example TLDs - use @example.com for synthetic addresses. Max 4 admins limit still applies; teardown must delete TEST_ admins. Login testids used: login-email, login-password, login-submit. Nav testid: nav-my-account. Profile testids: profile-name, profile-email, save-profile.
verified_behaviors: Name-only update succeeds and persists via /auth/me
Email update succeeds, is lowercased, new email logs in, old email is rejected
Email conflict with existing user returns 400 'already in use'
Updating to own email is a no-op success
UI: profile fields pre-fill from logged-in user, Save Profile shows success toast, Change Password + 2FA sections remain rendered
UI: freshly created admin can log in and self-service change email without superadmin
